title: They Might Be Giants bring humor, horns, and plenty of heart to House of Blues
author: Perry Eaton
bullets: They Might Be Giants performed two marathon sets at the House of Blues in Boston, featuring classics and new songs.
The duo, joined by an eight-piece band, highlighted their 1996 album 'Factory Showroom' and newest release 'The World Is to Dig'.
The band delivered two encores, including fan favorites 'Istanbul (Not Constantinople)' and 'The End of the Tour'.

title: The U.S. World Cup team’s biggest lineup debate has Mass. ties at its core
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Freese and Matt Turner, both with Massachusetts ties, are competing for the U.S. World Cup starting goalkeeper position.
Freese, formerly of Harvard, has had current U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino's confidence, while Turner shows superior shot-stopping stats.
Bruce Arena suggests the possibility of rotating goalkeepers, an unusual strategy for the upcoming World Cup.

title: New England Aquarium to celebrate World Ocean Day with free waterfront events on Sunday
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The New England Aquarium will host free World Ocean Day events on Sunday from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. at Central Wharf.
Highlights include a life-sized humpback whale installation, indigenous fishing demos, and climate art exhibits on Boston's waterfront.
Free film screenings and shuttle services from Dorchester and Roxbury will be provided; pre-registration required through local clubs.

title: Review & setlist: 5 Seconds of Summer keep the fans at the center of an unforgettable show
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 5 Seconds of Summer performed at TD Garden on June 5, 2026, for their 'Everyone's A Star!' World Tour.
The concert featured a theatrical structure, fan interaction, and humor, enhancing the connection with over 12,000 fans.
The setlist was a mix of older hits and new material, with audience participation in songs and fan-voted selections.

title: What 17th-century advice seekers had in common with us
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein to host a discussion on historical advice on June 30 at Boston Public Library.
Event features historian Mary Beth Norton, author of a book on the first personal advice column.
Free event will explore advice trends from the 1600s to present, including enduring relationship issues.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for June 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'Creed' and 'Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse' to its June streaming lineup.
'Hoppers,' a new Pixar film, is streaming on Disney+ this month featuring Piper Curda.
Amazon Prime debuts 'Spider-Noir,' a 1930s gangster tale starring Nicolas Cage as a Marvel webslinger.

title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox, defeating them 5-3 at Yankee Stadium.
Gray pitched 6.1 innings, allowing eight hits and three runs, and improved his season record to 7-1.
Previously expressing dislike for the Yankees, Gray explained he never wanted to play in New York during his 2017-18 tenure.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer and created a stir in the Red Sox dugout at Yankee Stadium.
Contreras collided with Andruw Monasterio in the eighth inning and vented frustration on a Gatorade cooler.
Contreras leads the Red Sox with 13 home runs and is second in batting average at .299.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with hail and damaging winds are forecast for Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening.
Greatest risk for severe weather is in areas west and northwest of Interstate 95, affecting much of the state.
Storms may bring localized flooding and are expected to diminish by 10 p.m. as they move offshore.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased staffing at Nantasket Beach on Friday due to social media rumors, but reported no incidents.
Officials noted the beach was quieter than expected, with ample parking and available beach space on a hot June Friday.
Rumors suggested Carson Beach as a new location for the event, but no gatherings or arrests occurred there.
